nav.desktopNav: Desktop gets the right-side sticky navigation. Mobile keeps the same sections in a lighter compact strip.

hero.title: Build a readable benchmark path, from prompt to run.
hero.description: BenchForge is a simple, self-hostable workspace to compare models on the same test base. Create prompts, register models, assemble sessions, launch runs, and read results.

structured.title: Structured
structured.text: Every benchmark stays in a predictable object model.

replayable.title: Replayable
replayable.text: Sessions let you rerun exactly the same test base later.

readable.title: Readable
readable.text: The path from prompt to result stays visible at a glance.

prompts.subtitle: Create the content to test
prompts.description: Centralise your prompts, variants and execution rules to keep a clean, reusable base.

models.subtitle: Register the engines
models.description: Register models, endpoints and parameters to compare multiple providers or configurations.

sessions.subtitle: Assemble the scenario
sessions.description: Combine prompts, models, candidates and judges into a clear, replayable benchmark session.

runs.subtitle: Launch and measure
runs.description: The session produces a run: execution, tracking, arbitration and result reading to decide quickly and accurately.

howToUse.title: A simple path from idea to evaluation.

step01.title: Prepare the prompts
step01.body: Start by writing the problem, constraints and variants to compare. BenchForge keeps everything structured.

step02.title: Connect the models
step02.body: Add one or more models, local or remote. You can then measure the differences on the same test base.

step03.title: Compose a session
step03.body: A session links prompts, models and evaluation rules. It is your benchmark plan, not just a list of items.

step04.title: Launch a run
step04.body: The run executes the session, collects responses and prepares result reading to compare cleanly.

startingPoint.title: Create prompts first, then let the rest of the pipeline follow.
startingPoint.description: The project is intentionally ordered. If the prompt layer is clean, the model registry, session builder and results stay easy to understand.

readyToLaunch.title: Compose a session and launch a run when the setup is stable.
readyToLaunch.description: Sessions wrap the benchmark. Runs execute it. Result: reproducible comparisons, easy to audit and share.

footer.text: BenchForge keeps the benchmark path visible from the first prompt to the final run.

hero.title: Prompt Library
hero.description: Manage reusable prompts, keep categories consistent, and archive old entries without deleting benchmark history.

filters.narrowLibrary: Narrow the library to one family of prompts.
filters.addTagsHint: Add tags to refine the library, or remove them to broaden it.

table.name: Name
table.seedingHint: Built-in prompts are seeded automatically on first load. Refresh if the library is still empty.
table.noMatchingPrompts: No prompts match the current filters.

editor.description: Create a reusable prompt or refine an existing one without leaving the library view.
editor.categoryHint: Select the closest prompt family used in the library.
editor.descriptionHint: Example: "Short benchmark brief displayed in the library."
editor.descriptionPlaceholder: Short benchmark brief displayed in the library.
editor.systemPromptHint: Example: "You are a precise analyst who writes concise answers."
editor.systemPromptPlaceholder: You are a precise analyst who writes concise answers.
editor.userPromptHint: Example: "Summarize the following text in 5 clear bullet points."
editor.userPromptPlaceholder: Summarize the following text in 5 clear bullet points.
editor.evalNotesHint: Example: "Check factual accuracy, structure and concise tone."
editor.evalNotesPlaceholder: Check factual accuracy, structure and concise tone.
editor.activeHint: Keep this enabled when the prompt should remain selectable in future sessions.

hero.title: Model Registry
hero.description: Manage candidate and judge profiles across remote APIs and local runtimes, with masked secrets and direct endpoint validation.

filters.selectRoles: Select one or more registry roles.
filters.narrowProvider: Narrow the registry to one provider.
filters.filterRuntime: Filter for remote or local model profiles.

table.noMatchingProfiles: No model profiles match the current filters.

warning.secretMissingDetail: This remote model cannot be used until a secret is configured.

editor.description: Create a new shared model profile or adjust an existing one from a dedicated editor.
editor.roleHint: Choose Candidate for generation, Judge for scoring, or Both.
editor.providerTypeHint: Pick a known provider or type your own value. Example: "openai", "google", "mistral", "groq", "deepseek", "huggingface" or "ollama".
editor.apiStyleHint: Recommended options are driven by the provider. You can still type a custom style if you know what you are doing.
editor.runtimeTypeHint: Choose Remote for API calls or Local for operator-driven execution.
editor.endpointUrlHint: Auto-filled from {{provider}}. You can still override it manually if your deployment uses a custom URL.
editor.modelIdentifierHint: Suggested from {{provider}} docs. You can select one or type any custom identifier.
editor.secretHintLocal: Local runtime does not require a secret.
editor.secretHintRemoteMissing: Remote models need a secret before they are usable.
editor.secretHintStored: Leave empty to keep the existing secret unchanged.
editor.secretHintDefault: Remote models need a secret before they are usable.
editor.secretPlaceholderKeepStored: Leave blank to keep stored secret
editor.secretNoLocalNote: No secret is needed for local runtimes.
editor.secretRemoteMissingWarning: Remote models without a secret are marked unusable until one is set.
editor.secretStoredNote: A secret is already stored. Leave this blank to keep it unchanged.
editor.notesHint: Example: "Use for fast draft generation on short prompts."
editor.notesPlaceholder: Use for fast draft generation on short prompts.
editor.localLoadHint: Example: "Launch Ollama, load the model, then click Confirm ready."
editor.localLoadPlaceholder: Launch Ollama, load the model, then click Confirm ready.
editor.activeHint: Disable this if the profile should stay in history but no longer appear in new session setup.

hero.title: Sessions
hero.description: Assemble prompts, candidate models, and a judge into reusable benchmark sessions ready for launch.

list.title: Sessions List
list.description: Browse and manage benchmark session definitions.
list.empty: No sessions found. Create a benchmark session with seeded prompts and registered models to launch your first run.

actions.configureDescription: Open the step-by-step selection flow for prompts, candidates, and judge.
actions.editDescription: Edit the session name, description, status, candidate limit, and rubric version.
actions.launchDescription: Create and start a new benchmark run from this session configuration.
actions.duplicateDescription: Clone this session with its current prompts, candidates, and judge selections.
actions.archiveDescription: Archive this session so it disappears from the active list without deleting history.

editor.description: Create a benchmark session or update the selected one without leaving the setup screen.
editor.descriptionHint: Example: "Compare three models on product update summarization."
editor.descriptionPlaceholder: Compare three models on product update summarization.
editor.statusHint: Use Draft while configuring, then Ready when the session can be launched.
editor.maxCandidatesHint: Choose how many candidate model slots this session can accept.

prompts.label: Prompts
prompts.description: Choose the prompts included in this benchmark session.

candidates.label: Candidates
candidates.description: Attach up to {{max}} candidate models for this run configuration.

judges.label: Judge
judges.description: Assign the judge model responsible for evaluation.

hero.title: Runs
hero.description: Track benchmark progress, inspect candidate outputs, and guide local execution when an operator needs to take over.

card.title: Runs List
card.description: Browse immutable run snapshots and reopen their operational detail.

empty.title: No runs found
empty.description: Launch a benchmark from the Sessions page to create the first immutable run snapshot.

preview.title: Report Preview · {{name}}
preview.description: Embedded PDF preview of the generated benchmark report.

phase1.executionDescription: Each candidate LLM executes the entire prompt list. Endpoint models run directly; local models go through an LM Studio handoff.
phase1.responsesDescription: One row = one prompt executed by one candidate. Click a response to open its detailed inspection in a large modal.

table.empty: No responses recorded yet. Candidate execution has not produced persisted outputs yet.

phase1.localCandidate: Local candidate. Load it in LM Studio, confirm readiness, then start the current handoff.
phase1.localCandidateDesc: Local candidate. Load it in LM Studio, confirm readiness, then start the current handoff.
phase1.remoteCandidate: Remote candidate. Endpoint execution can run in parallel with every other remote candidate.
phase1.remoteCandidateDesc: Remote candidate. Endpoint execution can run in parallel with every other remote candidate.
phase1.queuedLocal: This local candidate is queued and will expose its handoff details when it becomes the active local model.
phase1.noLocalInstructions: No local load instructions were provided.

phase2.title: Phase 2 · Judging
phase2.description: This phase unlocks only when all candidates have completed all prompts.
phase2.lockedDescription: Candidate execution must finish first. {{completed}}/{{expected}} candidate responses are completed.

noBatches.title: No judge batches yet
noBatches.description: Phase 1 is complete. Start judging manually to create one judge batch per prompt.

selectBatch.title: Select a judge batch
selectBatch.description: Choose one batch to inspect rankings, criterion scores, and written feedback.

noEvaluation.title: No parsed judge evaluation yet
noEvaluation.description: The selected batch has not completed.

phase3.title: Phase 3 · Aggregation And Report
phase3.description: After judging, you can aggregate the final scores and generate HTML and PDF artifacts.
phase3.lockedDescription: Judging must complete successfully for every batch before report generation is available.

rankingMatrix.title: Prompt Ranking Matrix
rankingMatrix.description: One column per prompt. The best score for each prompt stands out visually in the matrix.

noRanking.title: No prompt ranking yet
noRanking.description: Prompt-by-prompt ranking becomes available after judge batches complete.

noSummary.title: No aggregated summaries yet
noSummary.description: Aggregation runs after judging completes successfully.

response.title: Selected Response · {{prompt}}
response.description: Detailed inspection of the selected response, with the payload, normalized text and execution metrics.

ranking.noRankingDesc: Prompt-by-prompt ranking becomes available after judge batches complete.
ranking.title: Prompt Ranking Matrix
ranking.description: One column per prompt. The best score for each prompt stands out visually in the matrix.

summary.noSummaryDesc: Aggregation runs after judging completes successfully.

judging.noBatchesDesc: Phase 1 is complete. Start judging manually to create one judge batch per prompt.

feedback.selectBatchDesc: Choose one batch to inspect rankings, criterion scores, and written feedback.
feedback.noEvaluationDesc: The selected batch has not completed.
